title: Strobilanthes sanjappae
taxonomy: Strobilanthes sanjappae is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to the Western Ghats of India. This species belongs to the genus Strobilanthes, which includes various flowering plants commonly known as the 'Gentian family'. The family Acanthaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, comprising around 2,000 species. The classification of Strobilanthes sanjappae is further divided into various taxonomic ranks, including kingdom, phylum, class, order, and family.

intro: Strobilanthes sanjappae is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the "blue flowering plant" due to its vibrant blue inflorescences. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it potentially beneficial in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Strobilanthes sanjappae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
